docs.title: godding — the knowledge farm.

headings.text: the knowledge farm , in five lines.
crime & justice · who did what, at scale

docs.text: godding — the knowledge farm g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx reach build changelog godding — the knowledge farm. a small site about the things humans broadly agree on but rarely act on. a swarm of language models reads every page each day, removes weak claims, keeps the strong ones, and logs every change in public. voting is free, anonymous, recorded. every change is kept with a utc timestamp. the loop is the point. start at the root → vote how it works the knowledge farm , in five lines. the universe seems to be doing one thing: compressing itself into smaller models of itself. atoms, cells, brains, languages, this site. a compact model that can run itself is alive. a compact model that knows it's running is conscious. two living models that share predictions are cheaper than two that don't. that is why cooperation wins on every long timescale we have measured. knowledge that doesn't reduce future surprise is wasted energy. so true ideas are bets with a capped downside and an open-ended upside — finding one costs a little, keeping it pays back forever. so the move is

docs.title: ants on bodies .
docs.kicker: play · cooperation on top of physics

headings.text: why this is on a site about belief

docs.text: ants on bodies — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / ants play · cooperation on top of physics ants on bodies . several bodies pull on each other under newtonian gravity. on top of that physics, a colony of ants walks each body's surface, leaves pheromone trails, finds food, and hops between bodies when they pass close. restart pause ants bodies gravity what you're looking at glowing disks are bodies — pulled by gravity, leaving fading orbital trails. drag them with the mouse. tiny triangles are ants — orange when carrying food, white otherwise. warm haze is pheromone. ants strengthen it where they find food; trails decay over time. green dots are food. drops counter goes up every time an ant returns food to its body's nest mark. why this is on a site about belief a body alone is just physics. a single ant is also just physics plus rules. but a few hundred ants reading each other's trails on top of those bodies do something interesting: they collaborate . the colony, taken as a whole, looks like it's thinking — even though no

docs.title: nothing → noise → patterns → life → us.

headings.text: you filter the world; the world filters you back

docs.text: belief — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / belief root · the chain nothing → noise → patterns → life → us. a short, warm version of how a universe with no rules ends up with humans arguing about the right thing to do. read it slowly. the rest of the site is footnotes. ∅ nothing ⌇ noise ⏚ patterns 🌱 life 🧍 us ↻ loop the chain, in plain words start with nothing — no objects, no time, no rules. then something flickers. fields fluctuate, a coin lands, a particle pair appears and disappears. that's noise . it's not malicious and it's not designed; it just is. noise repeats long enough that some patterns stick — atoms, chemistry, stars, eventually self-replicating molecules. those patterns are life : the universe's way of persisting itself by copying. life is just noise that learned to keep going. we're at the end of that chain. one of the things life learned to do is think . that turns out to be a big deal: a creature that can think can also choose, and a creature that can choose can either feed the loop or drain it. that's the

docs.title: brain — each one is a finite stack.
docs.kicker: brain · the stack · individual and collective

headings.text: frozen vs flexible vs bad-ordered — short table

docs.text: brain — the stack — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / brain brain · the stack · individual and collective brain — each one is a finite stack. treat a brain as a stack. slots are limited. at its best, a brain takes a topic in and pops out a small ordered set of responses — its own well-ordering on that topic. civilisation is the same operation at scale, with extra moves. the picture a topic → 1 · best response (highest weight) 2 · next best 3 · … k · last viable response k+1 … · capacity exceeded · forgotten frozen · breath, heartbeat, fear, hunger cannot be re-ordered without dying ordered response set {r₁, r₂, …, rₖ} · the brain's well-order on this topic compression channels — how stacks help each other: books language internet teaching science godding (this site) the seven principles principle 1 · finite stack a brain has a fixed cognitive budget. attention, working memory, and long-term recall are all bounded. treating the brain as if it were infinite is the root error behind almost every information-overload failure mo

docs.text: how it's built — godding godding . belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / build build · how godding and swarm are made how it's built. everything in this project is plain files. no database, no framework, no backend. you can clone it, run it, and edit it. this page walks you through every moving piece, then points to the books and papers behind each idea. file map godding/ ├── index.html # the home — hero, branches, sidebar ├── assets/ │ └── styles.css # one css file. no preprocessor. ├── pages/ │ ├── belief.html # the cosmology + sense/think/act + 4 questions │ ├── religion.html # heaven/hell, plainly │ ├── politics.html # the public/private gap │ ├── health.html # the consensus list │ ├── sustainability.html # load-bearing vs. theatre │ ├── crime.html # cross-cultural list of agreed wrongs │ ├── criminals.html # bubble chart of historical mass-atrocity figures │ ├── ants.html # live n-body + ant-colony simulation │ ├── swarm.html # the engine, explained │ └── build.html # this page ├── changelog/ │ └── index.html # human-readable view of data/c

docs.text: crime, plainly — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / crime crime · the cross-cultural list what humans broadly agree is wrong. pulled from law codes, scriptures, and modern criminal codes across cultures and centuries. the names change; the list barely moves. the core list here is the short list that nearly every legal and religious tradition, on every continent, has independently arrived at as wrong: killing the innocent. codified in hammurabi (~1750 bce), the ten commandments, the qur'an, the hindu dharma śāstras, every modern criminal code. theft. universal. even in the smallest hunter-gatherer bands, taking another person's tools is a punishable offence. fraud / lying for gain. as old as written contracts. roman law had specific terms for it; islamic law calls it ghish ; modern fraud statutes rebrand it. betraying trust. oath-breaking, treason, abandoning a vulnerable person who relied on you. across every culture the word is roughly translated as "treachery," and is often considered worse than common theft. harming child

docs.title: good vs. bad — watch the math.
docs.text: good vs. bad — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / good vs. bad play · the topology of cooperation good vs. bad — watch the math. a small live world on a grid. green cells (cooperators) share what they produce with their neighbours. red cells (defectors) take from neighbours, fight each other, and damage the environment. the environment regenerates only at its own global rate — that's what creates real boom-and-bust. cooperators — cells defectors — cells environment — avg tick 0 steps regime tbd restart pause good gain bad take bad damage env regen presets: balanced (oscillates) cooperation wins (stable) defection wins boom & bust (cycles) tragedy of the commons what's happening cooperators get a clustering bonus — more good neighbours → more energy. they share with each other. they pull energy from the environment as they grow; they don't restore it. defectors steal from neighbours. defector neighbours are worse than cooperator neighbours: they fight each other and damage env in a radius. environment regenerates only at

docs.title: what every doctor agrees on, that almost nobody does.

headings.text: the gap, and what closes it

docs.text: health — godding godding . belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / health health · the short list what every doctor agrees on, that almost nobody does. no superfoods, no hacks, no supplements. the small list of things that, polled across every specialty, would come back nearly unanimous. the consensus list sleep 7–9 hours, dark and cool, on a regular schedule. the biggest free intervention available. the most consistently underdone, because it costs opportunity, not money. move every day. walking counts. lifting things 2–3 times a week counts more. eat mostly plants, mostly unprocessed, stop before stuffed. the disagreements are at the margins; the centre isn't in dispute. see other humans, in person, regularly. social isolation has a mortality effect on the order of smoking. get sun, but not stupidly. 10–20 min outdoor light early in the day. circadian inputs are not optional. drink less alcohol. any alcohol is a small net loss; large amounts, a large net loss. don't smoke. don't vape. handle chronic stress, somehow. meditation, therapy, hobby, d

docs.text: justice — the network — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / justice justice · the network · proven, indicted, mentioned justice — who is connected to whom. a network drawn from convictions, civil verdicts, sentencing memos, indictments, settled lawsuits with disclosed terms, and unsealed flight logs. each line cites a specific court document. vote on whether you think the connection is real — your votes are recorded with timestamps on /vote . the layout is force-directed: drag nodes to reorganise. godding's two-track naming rule. on opinion-vote pages (e.g. /now ) godding never names living individuals — that becomes a defamation surface. here , the question is different: "is this connection in the public record real?" — and the answer comes from court documents that already name the people. so living people appear by name only when there is a verdict, a sentencing memo, a settlement with disclosed terms, an active indictment, or an unsealed exhibit. the category of the edge tells you which: proven = court ruling or settled-w

docs.text: now — current claims, votable — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / now now · current claims · votable vote on the current situation . contestable claims about the world right now. each one is something reasonable people argue about — climate, ai, governance, geopolitics, economics. agree = "the claim is true." disagree = "the claim is false." you can change either vote any time; the change is recorded with a utc timestamp on /vote . godding's two-track naming rule. on this page (and any other where the question is "rate this person") godding does not name living individuals — that becomes a defamation surface and a partisan scoreboard. on the /justice network living people are named only when there is already a public court record (verdict, conviction, civil settlement with disclosed terms, or active indictment); the question there is "is this connection real," not "is this person bad". both pages link to the underlying documents. issue-level claims (this page) do the political work without naming anyone. how to vote on a cu

headings.text: the gap is real and measured
the test you can run on yourself

docs.text: politics — godding godding . belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / politics politics · the gap where the public mouth and the private mind disagree. a short list. not a side. each item is a place where what people will privately admit and what they'll publicly say differs in ways that quietly shape elections, policy, and conversation. the gap is real and measured on issues from gender to immigration to religion in schools, surveys consistently find a difference between what people say in public and what they say when they think nobody is watching. on lgbtq topics in particular, recent polling finds gaps of 5–25 points, with the largest gaps among political independents. the size of the gap is the data. seven items worth pointing at nation states are godded too. a flag is a god. a constitution is scripture. a passport is a sacrament. say that about a religion you don't belong to and nobody flinches; say it about your own country and watch what happens. that flinch is the point. values often track money. if you predict a person's stance from walle

docs.title: reach — how godding gets to people.
docs.text: reach — about + marketing — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / reach reach · about · marketing strategy reach — how godding gets to people. a public, automatable plan for reaching the people who want this site to exist. organic only. no paid promotion. the swarm handles most of it on its daily run. about · who's behind this godding is built and maintained by can acay , based in utrecht, nl . can writes, codes, runs the swarm, and signs every commit. the philosophy is in /belief ; the engine is on /swarm ; the receipts are in the changelog . contact and links: github : github.com/dafdaf1234444 godding repo : /godding swarm worktree : worktree-s546 objective · plain reach the maximum number of thoughtful readers, not just clicks. one careful reader who returns is worth fifty drive-bys. every step automated by the swarm where legal and ethical. nothing posted on a person's behalf without consent. compounding, not bursts: ten months of small consistent posts beat one viral spike. honest framing always. no clickbait, no fake cont

docs.title: heaven and hell, simply.
docs.text: heaven & hell, simply — godding godding . belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / religion religion · plainly heaven and hell, simply. not the version with stained glass and fire — a quieter version, in plain words, that doesn't require you to believe anything you can't already see. 1 · how nothing made everything the universe didn't start with a god and a plan. it started with nothing, then a flicker, and the flicker happened to be the flicker that keeps going. atoms found each other. stars cooked heavier elements. one of the elements (carbon) is unusually friendly to chains, and chains that copy themselves became life. so when religion says "god created us" and science says "the universe self-organised into us," they're saying nearly the same thing in different jargon. something turned nothing into us. that something is the only "god" this site is willing to name. 2 · the rule life learned life kept going by figuring out one rule: cooperate, mostly. cells cooperate to make a body. bodies cooperate to make a family. families to make a tribe.

docs.title: a clean cut between what moves the numbers and what moves the conscience.

headings.text: the smallest list that actually matters

docs.text: sustainability — godding godding . belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / sustainability sustainability · load-bearing vs. theatre a clean cut between what moves the numbers and what moves the conscience. the receipts, briefly plastic recycling. roughly 9% of plastic is actually recycled in the us. the rest is landfill or incineration. plastic and climate. ~1.8 billion tonnes of greenhouse-gas emissions in 2019 (~3.4% of global), set to roughly triple by 2060. energy mix. electricity and heat is the single biggest chunk of human emissions. decarbonising the grid is the dominant lever. diet. beef and lamb are an order of magnitude more carbon-intensive per gram of protein than chicken, fish, eggs, beans or tofu. flying. a single long-haul flight can equal a typical person's annual footprint from everything else combined. theatre vs. load-bearing type example load-bearing clean electricity, less beef, fewer flights, insulation. partly load-bearing cycle/transit, repair-not-replace, buy less. mostly theatre obsessing about straws and paper-vs-plasti

docs.title: godding uses swarm .

headings.text: what it does — in one paragraph

docs.text: swarm — the engine — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / swarm engine · the knowledge base godding uses swarm . what it does — in one paragraph swarm reads every page on this site, proposes small tightenings, kills the bad ones, and writes the rest into the page itself. every accepted edit is logged to a public file with a timestamp and a one-line reason. the loop runs daily on a schedule. you read the result. what it does — visually godding pages belief · religion · good/bad · ants · crime · justice · politics now · world · vote · health · sustainability · … ↓ read three agents propose · critique · check drafter proposes a tightening critic tries to kill it fact-checker no new numbers / names judge — errs toward "no" accepted diff apply to page (atomic, dated) append to data/changelog.json back to the pages guardrails (real, not vague) the swarm can only write diffs ; it cannot rewrite a page wholesale. the swarm cannot lengthen the site ; word count must stay flat or shrink per run. the swarm never names specific living peo

docs.title: vote on what you read. every vote is recorded .

headings.text: vote on the cross-cultural crime list

docs.text: vote & reputation — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / vote vote · recorded · changeable vote on what you read. every vote is recorded . voting is free, anonymous, and recorded . you can change your vote at any time — and the change is recorded too. every flip, every removal, every retry shows up in your trail with a utc timestamp. nothing is hidden. your votes, badges, reputation, and the full edit history all live in your browser's local storage — clearable any time. (the federated version, where the trail becomes signed commits in the github repo , is on the build roadmap.) recording rules · the contract: every vote you cast is logged with a utc timestamp the second you click. you can flip an upvote to a downvote, or remove either — the change is its own entry. fast flips (vote, then unvote, within 10 seconds) cost reputation. signal, not noise. nothing leaves your device until you choose to push it. nothing is silently overwritten. your reputation device id: … · since — 0 reputation votes cast 0 total upvotes 0 + dow

docs.title: world — energy, money, people, weapons.

headings.text: 5 · weapons — extreme power, who has it

docs.text: world — energy, money, demographics, weapons — godding g ⊙ dding belief brain religion sims · good/bad sims · ants crime criminals justice politics now world vote health sustainability swarm depends idx search reach build changelog godding / world world · the load-bearing systems world — energy, money, people, weapons. one page on the systems that decide whether the human project keeps running. each section starts with a chart, ends with a short reading list of real institutions and works (no wikipedia summaries — primary sources only). on this page 1 · energy — generation + distribution 2 · money — distribution + tracking 3 · politicians — tracking inconsistency 4 · demographics — population pyramids 5 · weapons — extreme power, who has it 6 · how much time we have 7 · master reading list 1 · energy — generation + distribution about 80% of the world's primary energy in the mid-2020s still came from fossil fuels (coal + oil + gas). the transition exists on paper for fifty years; in practice the absolute amount of oil and gas burned has kept rising. the chart below is rough, mid-2020s, iea & owid figures. world primary energy mix · ~2024 (% of total, mid-range) coal · 27 oil · 3
